title: Dianthus Pinks Grans Favourite Hardy Perennial 1 x 9cm Nursery Potted Plant by Thompson and Morgan
features: A gorgeous candy pink and cream coloured dianthus!
Ideal for patio pots, boarders and cut flower displays
description: Dianthus 'Gran's Favourite' Flowers This isn’t a favourite for no reason! This gorgeous dianthus has a creamy white centre of frilly petals, decorated with a lace of beautiful candy pink. The delicate flowers will provide a clove-like scent to your patio pots, boarders; complimented by the silver-green hardy foliage. ‘Grans Favourite’ will perform from late May into early October, where the long lasting flowers can be cut for indoor arrangements! Award-Winning Well-loved and a RHS AGM variety that is very popular, particularly for its white blooms and raspberry picotee edges, Scented Fragrant, clove-scented flowers to fill your garden! Long Flowering Period A prolific flowering period, meaning there will be plenty of blooms to spare for a vase indoors. How to Grow Dianthus 'Gran's Favourite' Pot up dianthus plants into pots of free-draining compost, and grow dianthus on in frost free conditions until large enough to plant outside. When all risk of frost has passed and plants are well grown, acclimatise them to outdoor conditions over 7 to 10 days. Plant dianthus outdoors in moist, well drained soil that has been enriched with plenty of well rotted manure or garden compost. Choose a position in full sun. Feed and water regularly until dianthus plants are fully established. Deadhead faded dianthus flowers frequently to encourage more blooms to be produced. Images shown are for guidance only of the expected results from plants upon maturity. Different growing conditions might vary results. Image may be included for illustration of supply but can vary due to seasonality changes (e.g. deciduous plants lose leaves in colder months)
